notes: Wilson hires Holmes to investigate the sudden dissolution of the Red-Headed League — an organisation that had been paying him four pounds a week to copy the Encyclopædia Britannica. Holmes deduces within moments of hearing the story that the League was a fiction designed to keep Wilson from his shop while John Clay tunneled toward the adjacent bank vault. The case is one-sided: Wilson contributes only his bewildered account; Holmes contributes the solution. They do not appear to interact again after the case is resolved.
